reasoning:
On June 22, 2025, Niemann's FIDE rank on Wikipedia is exactly #20. "As high or higher" means maintaining a rank in the Top 20 (i.e., Rank 1 to 20).

1. 2025-06-29: The Wikipedia Top 20 list strictly follows the official FIDE monthly releases, which come out on the 1st of every month. On June 29, the page will practically with certainty still reflect the June 1 list, which definitively shows him at rank #20.
2. 2025-07-22: The July FIDE list will be reflected here. Searches indicate Niemann has mostly played non-classical formats in June (like Titled Tuesday and Freestyle chess), meaning his classical rating likely stays frozen at 2736. Sitting precisely on the bubble at #20 implies a single rating shift from active players hovering nearby in the 2725-2745 range could either bounce him out (if anyone below him surges) or safely promote him to #19 (if an active player above loses points). Thus, the likelihood is near a coin toss, but slightly favorable due to his current positive momentum.
3. 2025-09-20 & 2025-12-19: Ranking movements grow progressively more chaotic over a multi-month span horizon. Niemann's classical rating has been highly volatile in recent years. Per standard forecasting principles, we assume regression toward the mean and inject maximal uncertainty as the horizon increases, anchoring the distant forecast dates cleanly onto 0.50.
